source.title: Hippocampal theta sequences reflect current goals.

confidence_rationale: Wikenheiser & Redish demonstrate that hippocampal theta-cycle place-cell sequences carry goal-relevant content -- the look-ahead distance of theta sequences scales with the distance to the rat's current goal. This is direct evidence that goal_latent is theta-locked and theta-cycle-resolved, which is one of the four content streams MECH-294 needs to bind. Confidence 0.74: direct unit-level rodent evidence, Nature Neuroscience, but the paper demonstrates only one of MECH-294's four streams (goal) being theta-locked; it does not address whether goal content is co-bound with action/risk/state content in the same theta cycle.
failure_signatures: The paper demonstrates goal-content theta-locking. MECH-294 needs goal + action + risk + state co-bound in one cycle. Single-stream theta-locking is necessary but not sufficient evidence.

Theta sequences here are place-cell sequences (location representations modulated by goal). MECH-294's action_proposal and risk_estimate streams are not place-cell content -- they are content sourced from BG and amygdala/ACC respectively. Whether those streams are theta-locked is not addressed.

The look-ahead-scales-with-goal-distance result is a content-modulation effect, not a binding-window effect. The paper does not test whether the goal latent is co-readable with other content types in the same theta-cycle window.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal theta-cycle place-cell sequences project ahead of the rat's current location, and the distance projected (look-ahead) scales with the distance to the rat's current goal. Look-ahead extends farther on journeys to distant goals, is predictive of the animal's destination, and equalises across journeys at goal arrival.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-294 takes the Wikenheiser & Redish result as direct evidence that goal_latent (one of the four streams MECH-294 needs to bind) is theta-locked and theta-cycle-resolved. The look-ahead modulation by goal distance also supports the broader claim that theta sequences are not mere clock signals but carry task-relevant content shaped by the agent's intentions.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper demonstrates a single content stream (goal-modulated place sequence) being theta-locked. MECH-294 needs four streams co-bound in one cycle. The single-stream demonstration is necessary but not sufficient. The paper also does not address whether action/risk/state content is jointly readable in the same theta window.
mapping.source_context: Direct CA1 unit recording in rats performing a value-guided decision-making task. Nature Neuroscience, 2015. Strong methodological standards; Redish lab is well-known for theta-sequence analysis.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Nature Neuroscience, direct rodent recording). Mapping fidelity moderate-high because goal_latent theta-locking is directly demonstrated for one of MECH-294's four streams. Transfer risk modest -- the result is single-stream and the joint-binding extension is not tested.
